mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains above 5% for multiple consecutive months, the cumulative lagged effect of restrictive monetary policy on corporate borrowing costs and consumer credit suppresses forward equity returns as tightening transmits through the real economy with a multi-quarter lag.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage_value=5.33 as of 2024-05-01, unchanged from prior month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS>5% regimes are not significantly lower than unconditional average (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window in restrictive-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month moving average trend in UNRATE continuously signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which erodes consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month regardless of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised_value=3.9 as of 2024-05-01, near multi-year lows
failure_condition: Correlation between UNRATE 3-month trend slope and forward 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p>0.05) across full 2005-2024 sample, or sign flips in more than 40% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS retail sales growth and HOUST housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signals robust near-term economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns; neither series alone captures this synergy since retail sales without housing follow-through often reflects transitory spending.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month change > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce positive 20-day forward returns in less than 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either single series (marginal Rank IC improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in INDPRO signals contracting industrial output and manufacturing sector stress, which precedes broader earnings downgrades and elevated recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter once the decline threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value=100.2434 as of 2024-04-01 showing downward revision from vintage 102.7623
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following INDPRO YoY<-1% triggers are not significantly negative versus unconditional baseline, or such triggers precede positive returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in PAYEMS monthly job gains crossing below a growth threshold signals an abrupt labor demand slowdown that markets price in rapidly, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within the following trading week as short-term repricing occurs.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below +100,000 after prior 6-month average above +200,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value=158543.0 revised down to 157608.0 as of 2024-05-01, indicating downward revision risk
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following PAYEMS deceleration triggers show no significant negative skew (p>0.10), or effect disappears after accounting for high revision risk noise in PAYEMS releases.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle tightening, the interaction between elevated FEDFUNDS and decelerating CPIAUCSL inflation implies rising real interest rates are peaking while disinflation validates a coming policy pivot; this joint condition produces positive forward S&P 500 returns as markets anticipate easing, an effect not present when only one series moves in isolation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change decelerates for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value=313.225 revised to 313.175 as of 2024-05-01
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward return rate under the joint condition is not significantly higher than the FEDFUNDS-only restrictive baseline, or Rank IC contribution of the interaction term is negative in more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in HOUST housing starts continuously reflects forward-looking builder and consumer confidence in future economic conditions, functioning as a leading indicator that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon irrespective of the prevailing macro regime.
failure_condition: Correlation between HOUST 6-month trend and 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over the full sample, or the relationship is unstable with sign reversals in more than 40% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a Sahm-rule-style UNRATE increase of 0.3pp+ over its 3-month low combined with simultaneous negative PAYEMS growth signals confirmed recession onset rather than transitory noise, triggering sharp negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week as markets rapidly reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month low AND PAYEMS month-over-month change turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised_value=3.9 as of 2024-05-01 remains near cycle lows
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to produce negative 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of historical occurrences, or the interaction offers no improvement in Rank IC versus the UNRATE-only Sahm signal.
